record_types.institutional-standard: Charters, principles, methodologies, research standards, and governance standards.
record_types.policy-legal-record: Privacy, terms, disclaimers, licensing, records, participation, and professional-boundary policies.
record_types.product-system-brief: Architecture, environment, product, service, module, and system briefs.

statuses.draft: Working record with no institutional authority.
statuses.under-review: Record undergoing editorial, technical, institutional, or legal review.
statuses.current-approved-record: Approved current authority within its defined scope.
statuses.current-living-document: Current authoritative HTML record expected to evolve through governed revision.
statuses.fixed-approved-snapshot: Approved immutable edition, normally represented by a PDF snapshot.
statuses.superseded: Former authority replaced by a named current record.
statuses.withdrawn: Record removed from current use without replacement authority.
statuses.historical-record: Preserved record with documentary but not current governing value.

authority_levels.institutional: Institution-wide identity, purpose, structure, or governing commitment.
authority_levels.methodology: Shared evidence, research, analytical, scientific, or reproducibility standard.
authority_levels.policy: Operational, legal, participation, licensing, privacy, or professional-boundary policy.
authority_levels.product: Current product, system, platform, environment, or module record.
authority_levels.historical: Preserved earlier record that does not govern current practice.

canonical_record_types.living-html: The maintained HTML record governs the current statement.
canonical_record_types.fixed-pdf: The approved PDF edition governs the named version.
canonical_record_types.repository: A repository file is the controlling technical record.
canonical_record_types.historical: The record is preserved only as historical evidence.
